home.headlineHtml:
I build systems
from silicon to full-stack.
home.headlineGlitch: I build systems from silicon to full-stack.
home.ledeHtml: Carnegie Mellon BS+MS Electrical & Computer Engineering student with a Robotics minor, building production software across React, Spring Boot, AWS, systems programming, networking, AI tooling, and security.
home.stack: Python · C++ · Java · TS · React · AWS
ticker: // build: HTTP server · AI authentication · Chrome extension

items.title: HTTP PSEUDO-STREAMING SERVER
items.description: HTTP/1.1 server built from scratch with low-level Python sockets, full method/status support, byte-range video streaming, and 200+ concurrent clients.

items.title: DEEP-LIVE-CAM AUTH SYSTEM
items.description: AI content authentication system with LSB watermarking, RSA-2048/4096 signatures, PKI automation, and Dockerized deployment across a 5-framework ML stack.

items.title: monoPix-scout
items.description: Chrome Manifest V3 extension that detects, classifies, and optionally blocks covert tracking pixels, beacon calls, and scripted network requests in real time.

items.title: YACA CHAT APP
items.description: Full-stack chat app with authentication, friend lists, REST APIs, responsive UI, websockets, and real-time group chat for 20+ users.

items.title: BUFFER POOL MANAGER
items.description: Thread-safe C++ buffer pool manager with LRU-K page replacement, background disk scheduling, promise I/O callbacks, and RAII page guards.

about.ledeHtml: I'm Adrian Muñoz, a Carnegie Mellon electrical and computer engineer who likes building across the stack: backend services, interfaces, infrastructure, embedded/systems code, and robotics-adjacent software.
about.bodyHtml: My recent work includes shipping a production admin UI at Atlassian, building AWS data-processing infrastructure at Amazon, evaluating AI coding models at Outlier, and building projects that span networking, cryptography, browser privacy, chat systems, and database internals.
about.quote: I like software that turns messy operational work into reliable systems people can actually use.
signals: BS+MS in Electrical & Computer Engineering, Robotics minor.
Production experience with React, Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, AWS, and Java services.
Comfortable moving from low-level systems to polished product UI.
Interested in full-stack systems, robotics, infrastructure, ML tooling, and security.

items.bodyHtml: Atlassian — developed a full-stack admin UI for release-track operations using React, Spring Boot REST APIs, and PostgreSQL; reduced admin workload by ~34 hours/week.
Outlier — created weekly coding prompts and validation checks, increasing model evaluation coverage by 17% using pandas/NumPy pass-rate measurements.
Amazon — designed a Java service for S3 file metrics and Lambda handlers linking DynamoDB data to QuickSight dashboards for ~11,000 ASIN records.
Carnegie Mellon University — BS+MS in Electrical & Computer Engineering, Minor in Robotics, GPA 3.40/4.00.
Built projects across HTTP networking, content authentication, browser privacy, real-time chat, and database buffer management.
Languages: Python, C, C++, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, R, HTML, CSS, MATLAB.

skills.caption: // frameworks: React, Spring, REST APIs, NumPy, PyTorch, ROS2, Node.js, Express, AWS, Django, TensorFlow, Flask.

contact.disclaimer: // open to software engineering roles spanning full-stack, systems, infrastructure, robotics, and applied AI tooling.
